Off Calendar Oct-06-2023 Text Ruling

Appearances (via Zoom):

Albert J. Garcia, Esq., Law Office of, (510) 848-5190, for Petitioner.

JASON J. AHN, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, (619) 738-9433, for Respondent.

RE: Petitioner's Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

Ruling is argued. At conclusion of argument, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR: 10619, T: (925) 226-8222; E: raquel@transcriptlegal.com. (302/CEK)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff RIM MARCINKUS' Notice of Hearing on Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to August 11, 2023, per the amended notice of motion.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/CK)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar May-09-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Order Staying Administrative Action
Off Calendar Jun-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Albert J. Garcia, Esq., Physician Advocacy, 510.848.5190, ajgmedlaw@yahoo.com, for Petitioner Rim Marcinkus, MD. (In Person appearance)
Martin Hagan, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, (619) 738-9433 for Respondent Medical board of California.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER RIM MARCINKUS' Motion For Order Staying Administrative Action.

PETITIONER RIM MARCINKUS Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Order Staying Administrative Action
Petitioner's motion for order staying administrative action is denied.

The court can stay respondent's decision if "the court is satisfied that the public interest will not suffer and that the licensed hospital or agency is unlikely to prevail ultimately on the merits." (CCP 1094.5(h)(1).)

Petitioner fails to demonstrate that respondent is unlikely to prevail on the merits. Petitioner contends that respondent violated Business and Professions Code section 2335 (c)(1) by not according proper weight to the administrative law judge's ("ALJ") decision. But that statute provides that the Board shall give great weight to the ALJ's "findings of fact" and petitioner's violations were undisputed. Respondent had the discretion to reject the ALJ's determination, find that petitioner acted dishonestly, and make its own decision regarding the appropriate discipline. (See Cadilla v. Board of Medical Examiners (1972) 26 Cal.App.3d 961, 966 ["[T]he propriety of a penalty imposed by an administrative agency is a matter vested in the discretion of the agency and its decision may not be disturbed unless there has been a manifest abuse of discretion."]; Govt. Code, Sec. 11517 (c)(2)(E) [Board may reject proposed decision]; Kifle-Thompson v. State Bd. of Chiropractic Examiners (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 518, 531 [discretion to reject ALJ's decision].)

Petitioner fails to show that imposition of Condition 17 (90-day suspension) was an abuse of discretion given petitioner's transgressions.

Petitioner also fails to show that Condition 20 (proof of accreditation) was an abuse of discretion. Petitioner was previously disciplined for using general anesthesia in an unaccredited surgery center. (AR 86; Hagan Decl., Ex. C.) Respondent could take note of that prior discipline in making its determination. (See Mepham v. State Bar (1986) 42 Cal.3d 943, 949 [noting "[attorney] has a substantial record of discipline which weighs heavily against him."].) Condition 20 was therefore not a "bolt from the blue" and there was no abuse of discretion as that condition was sufficiently tethered to petitioner's discipline history.

Counsel to meet and confer regarding adding additional language to the proposed order preserving Plaintiff's objections.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
